dictionary_sections.definition: Conversation Lane Digest is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation 360^\circ \times 360^\circ = 129{,}600 \text{ distinct angular nodes}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

Book rule: Use as narrative/explanatory conversational layer, clickable or sidecar style, separate from dictionary proof content. The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records 360^\circ \times 360^\circ = 129{,}600 \text{ distinct angular nodes}. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The supporting lemma is source locality: the selected relation appears in CONVERSATION_LANE_DIGEST_latest.md, and the adjacent context connects it to the title, domain, and surrounding argument. The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: Conversation Lane Digest extends the registry beyond the first thousand IDs by giving the new band a cited mathematical object, a text-rich dictionary page, and a derivation path attached to the exact source file.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: The figure for Conversation Lane Digest should place the canonical relation as the central object, show the source equations as incoming construction steps, and reserve the lower panel for the concept arc described in CONVERSATION_LANE_DIGEST_latest.md.
